methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ascend Elements is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Eric Gratz, currently valued at $3.0B post-money following $900M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 12 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, comprising 8 private firms and 1 public comparable (QuantumScape, LNZA), with observed peer valuations ranging from $700M to $6.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yields $3.6B. Peer-median analysis across the 12-company comparable set produces $3.4B; notable private peers include TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems, valued at $6.0B, $6.0B, $5.4B, and $5.0B respectively. Both methods demonstrate internal consistency within the $3.4B–$3.6B band.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.9B (low) to $4.1B (high), with a median of $3.5B. This implies the current $3.0B valuation sits modestly below the median, within the lower quartile of the derived range. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the peer sample given the wide dispersion ($700M to $6.0B) and stage heterogeneity within the comparable set, as well as path-dependent factors around capital deployment efficiency and technology commercialization risk typical of frontier-energy investments.
